mris.summary: Diagnostic of Beam's subscription cohorts, identifying that subscription churn was driven not by product quality or price, but by a failure to establish a daily consumption habit (Routine Friction) coupled with misallocated spend due to attribution errors.
observations: First-time subscribers experienced a 45% drop-off at the first billing cycle (Day 30).
A post-cancel survey showed 68% of users stated they 'had too much product left over,' rather than complaining about taste or efficacy.
Daily usage logs revealed that users who consumed the sleep blend at least 5 times in the first 7 days had a 92% retention rate at Day 90, whereas those who did not fell to 34%.
constraints: Primary: Routine Friction. The product packaging (scoop in jar) required active prep, introducing micro-barriers in the evening routine.
Secondary: Recovery Attribution Paradox™. Over-crediting click-based ads for acquisition while failing to observe that high CAC was subsidizing non-retaining cohorts.
mris.velocity: Habit velocity is negative if the packaging setup is manual. Transitioning to single-serve sachets with an integrated morning/evening trigger sequence is required to shift friction velocity to positive.
mris.validation: A 1,000-subscriber cohort test utilizing single-serve travel packs combined with a custom bedside nightstand stand demonstrated a 22% lift in Day-90 retention.

experiments.name: Bedside Sachet Holder
experiments.result: Day-90 cohort retention increased from 34% to 56%.

experiments.name: Evening SMS Ritual Triggers
experiments.result: Daily habit adherence self-reports rose by 14%.

mris.summary: Thesis requires users to perform significant cognitive work to self-diagnose and customize their nootropic blends. This mental load triggers the Executive Function Paradox, causing subscribers to cancel when they lack the mental energy to optimize their customization regimen.
observations: Subscribers are sent 4 distinct nootropic blends in their first month and are instructed to track their daily focus, energy, and mood.
Cohort logs indicate a 52% cancellation rate in Month 2.
Analysis of customer service tickets reveals that 40% of cancels mention feeling 'overwhelmed' by the tracking process.
constraints: Primary: Executive Function Paradox. Cognitive enhancers are purchased to solve executive dysfunction, yet the product requires high executive function to track and adjust.
Secondary: Personalization Decay. The perceived value of 'personalized formulas' degrades rapidly once the user selects a favorite and the customization UI continues to prompt adjustments.
mris.velocity: Feedback loop latency is currently 28 days (monthly box). Shortening feedback loops using digital micro-logs and auto-locking favorite formulas reduces tracking fatigue velocity.
mris.validation: Testing a streamlined 'favorite lock' checkout option in Month 2 (bypassing custom adjustments) showed an immediate reduction in cohort churn.

experiments.name: Interactive Monospace Telegram Log
experiments.result: Tracking completion rates improved by 22% in early test groups.

mris.summary: IQBAR's direct-to-consumer channel was heavily subsidizing retail trial margins. The brand was aggressively driving DTC acquisition to offset flat retail margins, masking structural weaknesses in unit profitability.
observations: DTC contribution margin was 28% while retail gross margin was 14% due to distributor chargebacks and slotting fees.
Marketing budgets were pooled, hiding the fact that 70% of ad spend was loaded on DTC, while offline stores were driving retail sales organically.
Overall company operating margin was trending negative despite 35% YoY top-line growth.
constraints: Primary: Margin Subsidy Trap. High DTC customer acquisition costs (CAC) were funded by cash flow, but the resulting customer LTV was too low to offset retail-level overheads.
Secondary: Price Inelasticity. Fear of retail shelf-space loss prevented price optimization.
mris.velocity: Margin depletion velocity was outpacing supply chain efficiency gains. A structural pricing correction was the only viable path to break the subsidy loop.
mris.validation: A targeted price increase of 8% on DTC bundles was implemented with zero impact on conversion rates, improving overall contribution margin to 36%.

experiments.name: DTC Elasticity Test (+8% Price Point)
experiments.result: Gross margin increased by 800bps with negligible change in DTC conversion rate (-2%).

mris.summary: Customized protein powder brands face high retention fatigue. Gainful's personalized flavor boosts require customers to manually mix flavor packets with unflavored bases, introducing routine friction and customization exhaustion over time.
observations: 42% of cancelations occur in Month 3, with comments indicating flavor boredom or friction in preparing customized mixes.
Cohort analysis shows users who ordered more than 3 flavor variations in a single shipment had a 30% lower retention rate compared to those who ordered only 1 flavor.
Average mixing time was reported at 4 minutes, leading to habit abandonment.
constraints: Primary: Routine Friction. The flavor-separate design adds a physical mixing step that increases preparation time and habit friction.
Secondary: Customization Exhaustion. Offering infinite choices leads to choice overload and subsequent cohort churn.
mris.velocity: Routine abandonment velocity accelerates as users complete their initial workout phases. Simplifying customization options stabilizes the habit loop.
mris.validation: Transitioning a test group to single-bag pre-flavored mixes reduced formulation changes and increased retention stability in Month 3 and 4.

experiments.name: Pre-Flavored Custom Blends
experiments.result: Cohort churn in Month 3 dropped by 15%.

experiments.name: Auto-Refill Shaker Cup Insert
experiments.result: Testing physical tracking cups to encourage hydration compliance.

mris.summary: Mushroom coffee replaces a highly ingrained morning coffee ritual. Everyday Dose successfully capitalizes on this, but suffers from Ritual Fragmentation when consumers travel or break their home routines, causing subscription build-up and cancellation.
observations: Subscribers have a high initial retention rate (80% Month 1 to Month 2), but a sharp drop of 35% in Month 4.
The primary cancellation reason is 'too much product' (54%). Customer surveys reveal travel and weekend trips break the consumption cycle, causing bags to stack up.
Subscribers who received a travel-tin accessory in their starter kit had 25% higher LTV.
constraints: Primary: Ritual Fragmentation. The product is physically tied to a kitchen counter setup (scoop, frother, jar). Routine breaks (travel, office days) lead to product backlog.
Secondary: Fixed Shipping Frequencies. 30-day shipping intervals do not match variable consumption patterns.
mris.velocity: Product accumulation velocity is positive for average users. Providing single-serve travel packets and a flexible delivery scheduler lowers product build-up velocity, reducing cancellation rates.
mris.validation: A structured experiment placing travel-friendly single-dose packs in Month-3 shipments immediately reduced cancellation requests by delaying consumption gaps.

mris.summary: Promix's clean formulas target serious athletes. However, its pricing structure allowed customers to buy heavy, single protein tubs with free shipping, compressing margins and causing operational losses on low-AOV shipments.
observations: Average Order Value (AOV) on first-time orders was $44, with shipping costs averaging $12 due to item weight.
Contribution margin on single-tub transactions was negative (-3%) after accounting for picker labor and carrier rates.
70% of ad spend was directed to single-tub SKUs, magnifying the operational loss.
constraints: Primary: AOV Compression. Pricing model and free-shipping thresholds did not incentivize multi-item orders.
Secondary: Shipping Overhead. Heavy powder containers incur high dimensional weight surcharges.
mris.velocity: Cash depletion velocity was correlated directly with growth in low-AOV customer acquisition. Shifting ad spend to multi-product starter bundles reverses this relationship.
mris.validation: Increasing the free shipping threshold to $75 and bundling powders with high-margin recovery items raised first-order AOV to $68.

experiments.name: Bundle-First Landers
experiments.result: First-order AOV increased by 54%, moving unit economics to profitable contribution.

experiments.name: Free Shipping Threshold Adjust
experiments.result: Operating margin improved by 620bps on direct store orders.

mris.summary: Naked Nutrition distributes heavily across Amazon and Shopify. This multi-channel footprint causes Attribution Dilution, where Google/Meta ad spend is credited entirely to Shopify, while a massive portion of the conversion occurs anonymously on Amazon.
observations: Ad accounts showed a blended ROAS of 1.4x, leading marketing teams to scale back budgets.
Post-purchase surveys showed 35% of consumers saw an ad on Instagram but chose to buy on Amazon Prime for free shipping.
Correlation models showed that for every $1.00 spent on Meta, Amazon revenue grew by $0.85, indicating substantial unmeasured channel crossover.
constraints: Primary: Attribution Dilution Trap. Relying on channel-isolated Shopify pixels misses the Amazon spillover, leading to premature campaign budget cuts.
Secondary: Multi-Channel Cannibalization. Pricing parity across Shopify and Amazon did not reward direct DTC brand relationships.
mris.velocity: Budget scaling velocity was artificially suppressed by a 40% under-reporting margin. Correcting attribution calculations allows the brand to capture market share.
mris.validation: Deploying cross-channel Media Mix Models (MMM) to track Amazon sales alongside Meta spend showed that true blended ROAS was 2.3x.

experiments.name: Cross-Platform MMM Integration
experiments.result: Corrected attribution models justified a 30% increase in ad spend, unlocking $1.1M in incremental run-rate revenue.

experiments.name: Shopify-Exclusive Bundling
experiments.result: Increased DTC share of wallet by 8% relative to Amazon.

mris.summary: Create pioneers creatine gummies, introducing a novel form-factor to a commoditized market. However, high production costs limit gross margin, creating a strict floor for Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) tolerance.
observations: Gummy production costs are 3.5x higher than equivalent powdered creatine, compressing gross margins to 55%.
A 20% discount on initial subscriptions compressed Month-1 contribution margin to $2 per bag, making the payback period 4 months.
Churn metrics indicate that 38% of subscribers do not reach Month-3 billing, meaning acquisition cost is never recovered on those users.
constraints: Primary: Pricing Elasticity. The product pricing ($45/bag) is already at the upper limit for creatine supplements, preventing simple price hikes.
Secondary: Margin Compression. Heavy reliance on discount-driven subscription acquisition limits immediate profitability.
mris.velocity: Payback velocity is slow due to high cost of goods sold (COGS). Shifting acquisition away from discount coupons toward value-add bundles is required to avoid cash constraints.
mris.validation: A pilot cohort acquired through high-margin accessories (custom shaker and storage pouches) shows a shorter payback period due to higher initial AOV.

experiments.name: Accessory Bundled Landing Page
experiments.result: Average Order Value rose by $12, reducing customer payback period to 2.2 months.

experiments.name: Tiered Volume Subscription Models
experiments.result: Testing multi-bag subscriptions to increase initial transaction margins.

mris.summary: Perelel offers prenatal vitamin subscriptions segmented by trimester. Because pregnancy has a fixed timeline, the customer is structurally forced to transition to new formulations, introducing high transition-stage churn (Lifecycle Exhaustion).
observations: Cohort churn spike of 28% occurs at the transition from Trimester 2 to Trimester 3.
Post-cancel interviews show that 60% of users who canceled during a transition stated they 'forgot to update their subscription info' or 'had boxes pile up.'
Subscribers who opted-in to automatic trimester progression SMS notifications had an 88% transition rate.
constraints: Primary: Lifecycle Exhaustion. The product transition points introduce active decision friction, forcing customers to re-evaluate their purchase monthly.
Secondary: Communication Latency. Email notifications regarding trimester shifts had a low open rate (22%), failing to prepare subscribers.
mris.velocity: Friction velocity is positive at each transition junction. Automating the transition logic via SMS confirmation turns decision friction into passive momentum.
mris.validation: Integrating SMS transitions with Shopify checkout reduced transition churn from 28% to 6%, significantly increasing lifetime value.

experiments.name: Post-Pregnancy Support Cohorts
experiments.result: Extended average customer lifecycle by 5.4 months through Mom Multi formulas.
